title: Limivasculum cespitosum

More specifically, it is classified as a species within the genus Limnas (though this is not confirmed), a genus of small flowering plants. Further research is needed to confirm its exact classification. The plant is likely native to a specific region, but its exact native range is unknown.
properties: Limivasculum cespitosum, commonly known as Wood Sorrel, has been used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing inflammation and protecting cells from damage. The plant is also said to have antimicrobial properties, making it effective against certain types of bacteria and fungi. Additionally, Wood Sorrel is thought to have diuretic and antiseptic properties, which can aid in the treatment of urinary tract infections and promote wound healing.
